subject: Science and Tech
stem: Which of the following is/are cited by the scientists as evidence/evidence for the continued expansion of the universe? 1. Detection of microwaves in space. 2. Observation of redshift phenomenon in space. 3. Movement of asteroids in space. 4. Occurrence of supernova explosion in space. Select the correct answer using the codes given below:
options: None of the above can be cited as evidence

solution.verdict: Option A is correct: 1 and 2
solution.detail: The Big Bang theory states that at some point of time the entire space was contained in a single point of very high density and temperature, from where the Universe has been witnessing expansion in all directions ever since. According to Hubble’s law, the expansion of the universe is such that the velocity at which the galaxy is moving away from the observer is increasing with time. Statement 1 is correct: Cosmic Microwave Background (CMB) is thought to be leftover radiation from the Big Bang, i.e., the time when the universe began. According to the theory, when the universe was born it underwent rapid inflation and expansion. (The universe is still expanding today, and the expansion rate appears different depending on observation). The CMB represents the heat left over from the Big Bang. Statement 2 is correct: American astronomer Edwin Hubble was the first to describe the redshift phenomenon (galactic redshift) and tie it to an expanding universe (galaxies are drifting apart). Redshift and Blueshift describe how light changes with movement of objects in space closer or farther from us. While moving away, the light is shifted to the red end of the spectrum, as its wavelength gets longer. While moving closer, the light moves to the blue end of the spectrum, as its wavelength gets shorter. Galactic Redshift is related to the expanding Universe concept. Statement 3 is incorrect: Movement of asteroids in space is due to gravity and it is not linked with the concept of expansion of the universe. Statement 4 is incorrect: The occurrence of supernova explosions (powerful stellar explosions in which a single star becomes as bright as 109 stars like the sun) helps in studying the distribution of elements throughout the universe. These elements travel on to form new stars, planets and everything else in the universe. But this is not considered as evidence for the continued expansion of the universe.

statements.text: Cosmic Microwave Background (CMB) is thought to be leftover radiation from the Big Bang, i.e., the time when the universe began. According to the theory, when the universe was born it underwent rapid inflation and expansion. (The universe is still expanding today, and the expansion rate appears different depending on observation). The CMB represents the heat left over from the Big Bang.

American astronomer Edwin Hubble was the first to describe the redshift phenomenon (galactic redshift) and tie it to an expanding universe (galaxies are drifting apart). Redshift and Blueshift describe how light changes with movement of objects in space closer or farther from us. While moving away, the light is shifted to the red end of the spectrum, as its wavelength gets longer. While moving closer, the light moves to the blue end of the spectrum, as its wavelength gets shorter. Galactic Redshift is related to the expanding Universe concept.
Movement of asteroids in space is due to gravity and it is not linked with the concept of expansion of the universe.

The occurrence of supernova explosions (powerful stellar explosions in which a single star becomes as bright as 109 stars like the sun) helps in studying the distribution of elements throughout the universe. These elements travel on to form new stars, planets and everything else in the universe. But this is not considered as evidence for the continued expansion of the universe.

subject: Science and Tech
stem: Biomass gasification is considered to be one of the sustainable solutions to the power crisis in India. In this context, which of the following statements is/are correct? 1. Coconut shells, groundnut shells and rice husk can be used in biomass gasification. 2. The combustible gases generated consist of hydrogen and carbon dioxide only. 3. The combustible gases generated from biomass gasification can be used for direct heat generation but not in internal combustion engines. Select the correct answer using the codes given below:

solution.verdict: Option A is correct: 1 only
solution.detail: Biomass gasification is a process that converts biomass (organic materials like wood, crop residues, and animal waste) into syngas (synthetic gas) by heating it in a controlled environment with limited oxygen or steam. Statement 1 is correct: Rice husk, groundnut shells, and coconut shells can all be utilised in the biomass gasification process. Statement 2 is incorrect: Biomass gasification involves burning of biomass in a limited supply of air to provide a combustible gas consisting of carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide, hydrogen, methane, water, nitrogen, along with contaminants like small char particles, ash and tars. Statement 3 is incorrect: Combustible syngas is frequently used as a fuel for internal combustion engines or as a step in the synthesis of other compounds. Consequently, the flammable gases produced by biomass gasification can be used for direct heat generation as well as in internal combustion engines.

statements.text: Rice husk, groundnut shells, and coconut shells can all be utilised in the biomass gasification process.

Biomass gasification involves burning of biomass in a limited supply of air to provide a combustible gas consisting of carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide, hydrogen, methane, water, nitrogen, along with contaminants like small char particles, ash and tars.

Combustible syngas is frequently used as a fuel for internal combustion engines or as a step in the synthesis of other compounds. Consequently, the flammable gases produced by biomass gasification can be used for direct heat generation as well as in internal combustion engines.

subject: Science and Tech
stem: Graphene has been frequently in the news recently. What is its importance? 1. It is a two-dimensional material and has good electrical conductivity. 2. It is one of the thinnest but strongest materials tested so far. 3. It is entirely made of silicon and has high optical transparency. 4. It can be used as ‘conducting electrodes’ required for touch screens, LCDs and organic LEDs. Which of the statements given above are correct?

solution.verdict: Option C is correct: 1, 2 and 4 only
solution.detail: Graphene is a single layer of carbon atoms arranged in a hexagonal honeycomb lattice, making it the thinnest, strongest, and most conductive material known. It has remarkable applications in electronics, energy storage, and nanotechnology due to its high strength, flexibility, and conductivity. Statement 1 is correct: Graphene, a two-dimensional, atomicscale, honey-comb lattice is an allotrope of carbon in which one atom forms each vertex. It is the basic structural unit of other allotropes, including graphite, charcoal, carbon nanotubes and fullerenes. Graphene has remarkably good electrical conductivity. Statement 2 is correct: It is often cited as about 100 times stronger than the strongest steel with a hypothetical thickness of 3.35Å(approx.) which is equal to the thickness of a graphene sheet. Carbon atoms are linked together in a single layer to form the hexagonal structure that makes up graphene. Graphene is so much thinner than paper (a million times thinner), that it is actually regarded as two dimensional. Statement 3 is incorrect: Graphene is a single layer (monolayer) of carbon atoms, not silicon, tightly bound in a hexagonal honeycomb lattice. It is an allotrope of carbon in the form of a plane of sp2-bonded atoms with a molecular bond length of 0.142 nanometres. Statement 4 is correct: Graphene is often produced as a powder and as a dispersion in a polymer matrix. This dispersion is supposedly suitable for advanced composites, paints and coatings, lubricants, oils and functional fluids, capacitors and batteries, thermal management applications, display materials, etc. Graphene is often cited as a promising material for use as conducting electrodes in devices like touch screens, LCDs, and organic LEDs due to its excellent electrical conductivity and high transparency

subject: Science and Tech
stem: Lead, ingested or inhaled, is a health hazard. After the addition of lead to petrol has been banned, what still are the sources of lead poisoning? 1. Smelting units 2. Pens and pencils 3. Paints 4. Hair oils and cosmetics Select the correct answer using the codes given below:

solution.verdict: Option B is correct: 1 and 3 only
solution.detail:
Lead poisoning is a toxic condition caused by the accumulation of lead in the body, typically through exposure to contaminated air, water, soil, or consumer products. It affects multiple organ systems, particularly the nervous system, and can lead to severe health issues such as cognitive impairment, developmental delays, and organ damage.
- Option 1 and 3 are correct: Even after the stricter regulations, major sources of lead pollution include mining, smelting, manufacturing, and recycling, with the production of leadacid batteries for automobiles accounting for over threequarters of global lead usage.
- Other common sources of lead poisoning include leadbased paint, contaminated soil, children’s toys and jewelry, drinking water from lead pipes, workplace and hobby hazards (such as mining and smelting), lead-glazed ceramics, pottery, leaded crystal, imported candy, and mini blinds.
- Option 2 and 4 are incorrect:
- Pens and pencils are incorrect as modern pencils mostly contain graphite, not lead, and pens use regulated ink formulations.
- Hair oils and cosmetics are incorrect because most cosmetic products are strictly regulated to prevent lead contamination, with only rare exceptions in some traditional items.

subject: Science and Tech
stem: Consider the following crops of India: 1. Cowpea 2. Green gram 3. Pigeon pea Which of the above is/are used as pulse, fodder and green manure?

solution.verdict: Option A is correct: 1 and 2 only
solution.detail:
Cowpea, green gram, and pigeon pea are all versatile crops used in various agricultural practices.
- Cowpea is a leguminous plant that serves as a pulse, fodder, and green manure. Its seeds are harvested for food, while the plant’s leaves and stems are used as fodder for livestock. Additionally, cowpea is effective as green manure due to its ability to fix nitrogen in the soil, enhancing soil fertility.
- Green gram, primarily grown for its seeds, is mainly used as a pulse and also as fodder for animals. Although it can contribute to soil health, it is less frequently used as green manure compared to other legumes.
- Pigeon pea is similarly multifunctional, used as a pulse for consumption, fodder for livestock, and green manure to enrich soil. It plays a crucial role in improving soil fertility by fixing nitrogen. Overall, all three crops serve important roles in sustainable agriculture, with cowpea and pigeon pea being more widely used as green manure than green gram. Note: However, UPSC didn’t consider Pigeon Pea as suitable fit and marked option (a) as correct

subject: Science and Tech
stem: To meet its rapidly growing energy demand, some opine that India should pursue research and development on thorium as the future fuel of nuclear energy. In this context, what advantage does thorium hold over uranium? 1. Thorium is far more abundant in nature than uranium. 2. On the basis of per unit mass of mined mineral, thorium can generate more energy compared to natural uranium. 3. Thorium produces less harmful waste compared to uranium. Which of the statements given above is/are correct?

solution.verdict: Option D is correct: 1, 2 and 3
solution.detail: Statement 1 is correct: Thorium composes 0.0006% of the earth’s crust whereas Uranium composes 0.00018% of the earth’s crust, where a substantial amount of Uranium is found in dissolved seawater. Thus, Thorium(Th) is comparatively far more abundant in nature than uranium(U). Statement 2 is correct: Uranium-233, produced from thorium, is generally considered to have higher energy output compared to natural uranium, with estimates suggesting it could produce significantly more energy per unit mass, though the exact ratio can vary depending on reactor design and conditions. Statement 3 is correct: Thorium-based reactors generate less long-lived radioactive waste compared to uranium-based reactors. The waste from thorium has a lower proportion of transuranic elements like plutonium, which are highly radioactive and pose disposal challenges."

subject: Science and Tech
stem: Which one of the following sets of elements was primarily responsible for the origin of life on the Earth?

solution.verdict: Option B is correct: Carbon, Hydrogen, Nitrogen
solution.detail: Biologists studying various forms of life on Earth have identified a common characteristic among all living organisms. The matter within every living organism on Earth primarily consists of four chemical elements: hydrogen, oxygen, carbon, and nitrogen. Option (b) is correct: Carbon, hydrogen, and nitrogen are the elements that were primarily responsible for the origin of life on Earth. These elements are fundamental in the formation of organic compounds, such as proteins, nucleic acids, and lipids, which are essential for life. The combination of carbon, hydrogen, and nitrogen forms the basis of amino acids, nucleotides, and other critical biomolecules involved in the origin of life on Earth.

subject: Science and Tech
stem: Consider the following statements: If there were no phenomenon of capillarity 1. It would be difficult to use a kerosene lamp. 2. One would not be able to use a straw to consume a soft drink. 3. The blotting paper would fail to function. 4. The big trees that we see around would not have grown on the Earth. Which of the statements given above are correct?

solution.verdict: Option B is correct: 1, 3 and 4 only
solution.detail: Capillarity (or capillary action) is the ability of a liquid to flow in narrow spaces, such as thin tubes or porous materials, without the assistance of external forces and even against gravity. It occurs due to the cohesion (attraction between liquid molecules) and adhesion (attraction between the liquid and solid surface), along with surface tension. Statement 1 is correct: The oil in the wick of a lamp rises due to the capillary action of threads in the wick. Absence of capillarity would certainly make it difficult to use Kerosene lamps. Statement 2 is incorrect: There is no capillary action in using a straw to consume the soft drink as the consumer applies additional pressure from the mouth to consume the soft drink. Statement 3 is correct: A blotting paper soaks ink by the capillary action of the pores in the blotting paper. The blotting paper will not be useful without the capillarity phenomenon. Statement 4 is correct: The root-hairs of plants draw water from the soil through capillary action. Therefore absence of capillarity phenomenon would adversely affect the growth of trees.

statements.text: The oil in the wick of a lamp rises due to the capillary action of threads in the wick. Absence of capillarity would certainly make it difficult to use Kerosene lamps.
There is no capillary action in using a straw to consume the soft drink as the consumer applies additional pressure from the mouth to consume the soft drink.
A blotting paper soaks ink by the capillary action of the pores in the blotting paper. The blotting paper will not be useful without the capillarity phenomenon.
The root-hairs of plants draw water from the soil through capillary action. Therefore absence of capillarity phenomenon would adversely affect the growth of trees.
